title: Monsoon enters Odisha; IMD issues orange warning for 13 districts - Odisha TV
full_text:
The monsoon entered Odisha, bringing rainfall to several parts of the state. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued weather warnings for north and south Odisha as rain activity continues across various districts.
An Orange Warning was issued for 13 districts due to the likelihood of rain, lightning and thunderstorms. The Regional Meteorological Centre forecast heavy rainfall in Malkangiri, Koraput, Nabarangpur, Rayagada and Gajapati districts.
Heavy Rain Forecast For Southern Districts
According to reports, wind speeds are expected to reach 50-60 kmph in some areas. While heavy rainfall was predicted for parts of southern Odisha, other regions are less likely to receive significant rainfall.
A Yellow Warning was also issued for all remaining districts of the state. The IMD advised people to remain cautious in view of the prevailing weather conditions.
Alerts Continue For Coming Days
The Meteorological Department also issued an Orange Warning for five districts for June 14. Light showers are expected in some areas on the day.
However, as per the IMD forecast, no rainfall is expected across Odisha on that day. The weather agency further indicated that the effect of El Niño is expected to persist.
